hypothesesCache.title: Unmitigated failure mode: Gold Surface Passivation by Low-Melting Films
hypothesesCache.summary: Failure mode "Gold Surface Passivation by Low-Melting Films" is documented without mitigation strategies in the graph.
reasoning: Reliability-focused KPI improvements require explicit MITIGATES relationships.
Absence of mitigation edges suggests open reliability research opportunity.
suggestedExperiments: Identify coating, dopant, or processing interventions linked to this failure mode.
Validate mitigation under accelerated stress conditions.

nodes.name: Gold Surface Passivation by Low-Melting Films
nodes.definition: A roasting-induced failure mode in which molten or near-molten reaction products coat gold particles and block access of cyanide solutions. This passivation increases gold losses to cyanidation tailings because the metal becomes chemically inaccessible.
nodes.text: Gold Surface Passivation by Low-Melting Films (пленки легкоплавких соединений на поверхности золота, пассивирование золота пленками, поверхностные пленки легкоплавких соединений)
aliases: пленки легкоплавких соединений на поверхности золота

relatedConcepts: Gold Surface Passivation by Low-Melting Films

hypothesesCache.summary: Failure mode "Heap Channeling" is documented without mitigation strategies in the graph.
reasoning: Reliability-focused KPI improvements require explicit MITIGATES relationships.
Absence of mitigation edges suggests open reliability research opportunity.
suggestedExperiments: Identify coating, dopant, or processing interventions linked to this failure mode.
Validate mitigation under accelerated stress conditions.

nodes.name: Heap Channeling
nodes.definition: Nonuniform flow of leach solution through preferential paths in a heap. It reduces contact with ore, worsens extraction uniformity, and is promoted by poor stacking, segregation, and fines migration.

hypothesesCache.title: Unmitigated failure mode: Clay Compaction
hypothesesCache.summary: Failure mode "Clay Compaction" is documented without mitigation strategies in the graph.
reasoning: Reliability-focused KPI improvements require explicit MITIGATES relationships.
Absence of mitigation edges suggests open reliability research opportunity.
suggestedExperiments: Identify coating, dopant, or processing interventions linked to this failure mode.
Validate mitigation under accelerated stress conditions.

nodes.name: Clay Compaction
nodes.definition: Loss of heap permeability caused by clay-rich material being compressed during stacking or equipment traffic. In the slice, this is a key reason for reduced gold recovery from clayey and high-carbonate ores and motivates lower heap heights.
nodes.text: Clay Compaction (уплотнение глинистых руд, уплотнение глинистой фракции)

suggestedExperiments: Define KPI_Target nodes for key performance metrics of Ti-Ta Coating.
Link synthesis parameters to KPI outcomes through property intermediates.

nodes.definition: Механическая характеристика жёсткости материала, определяемая как отношение нормального напряжения к соответствующей деформации в упругой области. Регулирование модуля упругости в ячеистых структурах важно для создания имплантатов с требуемой жёсткостью.

Биоинертное покрытие на основе β-фазного сплава титана и тантала, наносимое электровзрывным напылением, отличающееся низким модулем упругости и наноструктурированной поверхностью; предназначено для улучшения остеоинтеграции титановых имплантатов.
Твёрдость материала, измеренная на нанометровом масштабе (часто методом индентирования), отражающая локальные механические свойства поверхностных слоёв, покрытий и наноструктурированных материалов.

Свойство материала не вызывать отторжения или токсических реакций в биологической среде; биоинертные покрытия (например, Ti-Ta) минимизируют воспалительные процессы и способствуют интеграции имплантата.

attributes.evidence_quote: получить модуль упругости, сопоставимый с костной тканью человека
покрытия системы Ti-Ta, свойства которых ... нанотвердость, модуль упругости
биоинертные покрытия системы Ti-Ta

reasoning: Reliability-focused KPI improvements require explicit MITIGATES relationships.

evidence.quote: The activated carbon loss metric is a key indicator of attrition failure, which is investigated in later studies as a primary cause of material loss.

suggestedExperiments: Identify coating, dopant, or processing interventions linked to this failure mode.
Validate mitigation under accelerated stress conditions.

nodes.name: Activated Carbon Attrition
nodes.definition: Mechanical degradation of activated carbon during transport, pumping, and cyclic column transfer, producing fine fractions that are lost with process streams. The slice identifies low mechanical strength and resulting sorbent losses as a key limitation of carbon sorption in cyanide circuits.
nodes.text: Activated Carbon Attrition (механическое разрушение угля, истирание угля, измельчение сорбента в мелкие фракции)

nodes.name: Sodium Sulfide
nodes.definition: An inorganic sulfide depressant used in flotation. The slice describes it as a reagent that suppresses many sulfide minerals and sulfidized oxidized minerals by introducing sulfide and hydrosulfide species into the pulp.

nodes.name: Hydrophobicity
nodes.definition: Tendency of a surface or particle to resist wetting by water and preferentially interact with a gas phase. In flotation, higher hydrophobicity promotes bubble attachment and froth recovery.

nodes.name: Selectivity
nodes.definition: The degree to which a collector or surface process acts preferentially on one mineral phase over others. In the slice, selectivity is governed by the solidophilic group and by the specificity and strength of surface bonding.

nodes.name: Graphite
nodes.definition: Low-polarity carbonaceous mineral used as an example of a hydrophobic surface in flotation theory. It is not readily wetted by water and therefore behaves differently from silicate minerals.

nodes.name: Hydrophobicity
nodes.definition: Tendency of a surface or particle to resist wetting by water and preferentially interact with a gas phase. In flotation, higher hydrophobicity promotes bubble attachment and froth recovery.

nodes.name: Wettability
nodes.definition: Ability of a liquid to spread on and adhere to a solid surface. In flotation, wettability depends strongly on the polarity mismatch between the liquid and the mineral surface.

nodes.name: Gold
nodes.definition: A precious metal mentioned in the context of cyanide flotation suppression. Cyanides can dissolve gold and thereby lower gold recovery into the froth product, which is an important practical limitation.

nodes.name: Chemical Stability
nodes.definition: Resistance of a metal to chemical interaction with surrounding media, especially oxidation and formation of oxygen compounds. The slice identifies this as a defining property of noble metals.

nodes.name: Wettability
nodes.definition: Ability of a liquid to spread on and adhere to a solid surface. In flotation, wettability depends strongly on the polarity mismatch between the liquid and the mineral surface.

nodes.name: Surface Oxidation
nodes.definition: A degradation process in which dissolved oxygen reacts with a mineral surface, especially sulfide minerals, forming oxidized layers or species. This typically increases hydrophilicity and reduces flotation performance by weakening bubble attachment.

nodes.name: Amalgamation
nodes.definition: Historical extraction method in which gold, silver, or platinum is selectively dissolved into mercury to form an amalgam. After washing, squeezing, and mercury distillation, a crude precious-metal product is obtained and the mercury is regenerated.

nodes.name: Gold Amalgam
nodes.definition: Product of gold amalgamation consisting of mercury with dissolved gold and dispersed solid phases. It may contain gold in solution, gold–mercury intermetallic compounds, and residual solid particles, and can become brittle when contaminated with copper or iron.

attributes.evidence_quote: амальгамация – процесс, основанный на способности ртути … образовывать соединения металла с ртутью – амальгаму

nodes.name: Calcine Cyanidation
nodes.definition: Cyanide leaching of a roasted calcine product to recover precious metals after thermal pretreatment. In the reported process it follows arsenatizing roasting and is used to exploit the increased chemical accessibility of gold and silver in the roasted material.
nodes.text: Calcine Cyanidation (цианирование огарка, cyanidation of calcine, roast-calcine cyanidation)

nodes.name: Calcine
nodes.definition: The solid product obtained after roasting ore or concentrate under oxidizing or chlorinating conditions. In this slice the calcine is a porous, partially agglomerated arsenatizing-roast product that is subsequently subjected to cyanidation to recover gold and silver.

attributes.evidence_quote: В результате их цианирования удается извлечь 90–95 % содержащегося в них золота.
полученный огарок цианировали в сорбционном режиме
